title: stat command reports incorrect/negative value for number of memory bits
body:
## Steps to reproduce the issue

Test case
```
module dut( 
    input clk 
    ,input ce 
    ,input we 
    ,input [31:0] bwe 
    ,input [31:0] addr 
    ,input [31:0] data 
    ,output reg [31:0] q 
); 

    (*nomem2reg*)
    reg [33000:0] mem [0:65536] ;
    
    always @ (posedge clk) begin
        if (ce && we) begin
            mem[addr] = (data & bwe) | (mem[addr] & ~bwe); 
        end
        if (!ce) begin
            q <= mem[addr];
        end
    end

endmodule 
```

Yosys script
```
read -vlog2k dut.v
hierarchy -check -top dut
proc
stat
```

## Expected behavior

Correct (non negative) number in the statistics for ' Number of memory bits'.

## Actual behavior

```
5. Printing statistics.

=== dut ===

   Number of wires:                 34
   Number of wire bits:         495344
   Number of public wires:           7
   Number of public wire bits:     131
   Number of memories:               1
   Number of memory bits:       -2132180759
   Number of processes:              0
   Number of cells:                 18
     $and                            2
     $dff                            4
     $logic_and                      1
     $logic_not                      1
     $memrd                          2
     $memwr_v2                       1
     $mux                            4
     $not                            1
     $or                             1
     $pos                            1
```

I think this can be solved by making  the 'num_memory_bits' in passes/cmds/stat.cc unsigned (perhaps long).
